text.style: font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: normal; fill: rgb(34, 36, 42);
text.tspan: Segregation of duties (SoD), enforced throughout: a user can never approve their own requisition or PO (actingUserId != record.requesterId and != record.buyerId). Auto-approval within configured thresholds is the ONLY no-second-person path and is always logged with the limit and amount.
Buyer != PO approver; the same engine serves requisition, PO, award, supplier-onboarding, and payment-schedule approvals.

rect.style: fill: none; stroke-opacity: 0; stroke: white; stroke-width: 15px;
stroke-linecap: round; stroke-linejoin: round; stroke: none; stroke-width: 2px; fill: none;

path.style: fill: none; stroke-linecap: round; stroke-linejoin: round; stroke: rgb(34, 36, 42); stroke-width: 2px;

text.style: font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: normal; fill: rgb(34, 36, 42);
text.tspan: Threshold and nearest-bucket: effectiveApproverLimit = approvalLimit ?? DEFAULT_LIMIT; DEFAULT_LIMIT = 200000, configurable per tenant; a configured 0 stays 0. Comparison <= is inclusive (amount == limit auto-approves). Nearest-bucket selection keeps only approvers at the minimum
eligible limit so high-limit approvers are reserved for large amounts; tie-break least-loaded (urgency-weighted open completions, ASAP=4 / SameDay=2 / Within2Days=1 / Within1Week=0).

rect.style: fill: none; stroke-opacity: 0; stroke: white; stroke-width: 15px;
stroke-linecap: round; stroke-linejoin: round; stroke: none; stroke-width: 2px; fill: none;

path.style: fill: none; stroke-linecap: round; stroke-linejoin: round; stroke: rgb(34, 36, 42); stroke-width: 2px;

text.style: font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: normal; fill: rgb(34, 36, 42);
text.tspan: Auto-approval revert (RA e02 postValueSync): after any write, recompute total and reconvert base; only if payTerms OR paymentSchedules changed, revert auto-approved Finance/Management completions to AWAITING_APPROVAL. Manual approvals and REQ_DEPARTMENT/PROCUREMENT
stages are never reverted. Hook never aborts the write; always emits record.updated + SSE.
